content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 1 correctness issue: the new next-step hint capture path mishandles `bv`'s successful no-work response, so stale `next_step_hint.json` state can survive after the last bead is completed. The rest of the reviewed reconciliation flow looked consistent with the intended close/sync/update ordering.

## Amendments

1. Handle the successful `bv --robot-next` "no actionable items available" response in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:167-181` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:361-370` (the current parser shape is `src/adapters/bv_process.rs:345-353`). Right now `capture_next_step_hint` only accepts `NextBeadResponse { id, title, ... }`; when `bv` succeeds with the message-only JSON it falls into `BvFailed`, and `reconcile_success` deliberately leaves any existing `next_step_hint.json` untouched. After the final actionable bead closes, that preserves a stale hint that can still point at an already-completed bead. Treat the message-only success payload as an explicit "no next hint" result and clear/remove any persisted hint in that case; add a regression test for `{"message":"No actionable items available"}`.
   Rationale: I reproduced `bv --robot-next` in a workspace with no actionable issues: it exits 0 and returns JSON with `"message":"No actionable items available"` instead of `id`/`title`. The current implementation misclassifies that as a transient failure and retains stale state.
